# mkpub

> A Claude Code skill that writes the four files a repo needs before it goes public:
> README.md, LICENSE.md, SECURITY.md, and llms.txt.

mkpub is markdown, not code — there is nothing to build, run, or test. `SKILL.md` is a
router: it holds the rules that apply to every mode, then dispatches to one
`refs/protocol-*.md` per mode. To change what a mode does, edit its protocol. To change
something that applies everywhere, edit SKILL.md. The only binary assets are four figlet
`.flf` fonts under `refs/fonts/`.

The design constraint worth knowing before you change anything: mkpub is not allowed to
invent facts. No fabricated benchmarks, no guessed license, no unverified install command,
no badge URL that doesn't resolve. Anything not derivable from the repo gets scaffolded
with an HTML comment or asked about via AskUserQuestion. Most of the prose in the
protocols exists to enforce that.

## Docs

- [README](README.md): what mkpub does, install, how it works, FAQ
- [SKILL.md](.claude/skills/mkpub/SKILL.md): the router — hard rules, context-gathering, collision and scaffolding policy. Start here.
- [voice.md](.claude/skills/mkpub/refs/voice.md): the prose rules for generated READMEs. Read before writing any user-facing prose; the constraints change sentence construction, so applying them after drafting means rewriting.

## Reference

One protocol per mode. Each is self-contained — read only the one you need.

- [protocol-readme.md](.claude/skills/mkpub/refs/protocol-readme.md): `--readme`. The figlet header mechanics, the four bundled fonts, badgen rules, and the seven required sections.
- [protocol-license.md](.claude/skills/mkpub/refs/protocol-license.md): `--license`. How to ask, how to recommend by repo type, resolving the copyright line, MIT text inline and SPDX fetch for the long ones.
- [protocol-security.md](.claude/skills/mkpub/refs/protocol-security.md): `--security`. Resolving a real reporting channel, supported-version tables, and writing an honest Scope section.
- [protocol-llms.md](.claude/skills/mkpub/refs/protocol-llms.md): `--llms`. The format of this file, and why link descriptions carry the weight.
- [protocol-update.md](.claude/skills/mkpub/refs/protocol-update.md): `--update`. Diff semantics — what counts as drift, what's the user's wording and stays untouched.

## Optional

- [LICENSE.md](LICENSE.md): MIT
- [SECURITY.md](SECURITY.md): how to report a vulnerability privately
